projects.name: MONAD
projects.description: Compiled identity kernel. A WASM binary that answers queries about one person.

/bin/monad.content: WebAssembly module (wasm32) — the compiled MONAD kernel. The same core runs in the browser, under WASI, and natively. Run `verify` for its reproducible vault hash.

/home/keshav/notes/distributed-systems.t.content:
Distributed systems notes:
- Eventual consistency requires conflict resolution strategies (CRDTs, vector clocks)
- Kafka partitioning key design is critical for ordering guarantees
- Circuit breaker pattern prevents cascading failures in microservice meshes
- Rate limiting at API gateway vs service level — different tradeoffs

/home/keshav/contacts.content:
Reachable via:
  email: keshavashiya@outlook.com
  github: https://github.com/keshavashiya
  linkedin: https://linkedin.com/in/keshavashiya

/proc/cpuinfo.content:
architecture : wasm32
model        : MONAD kernel (compiled Rust)
cores        : 1 (single-threaded, no preemption)
clock        : injected by the host adapter — no fixed frequency
state        : stateless; every cold boot is identical

/mnt/vault/profile.content: The identity vault — compiled into the kernel at build time via include_str!. This file is its source. Its reproducible sha256 is what `verify` reports.

bootlog.message: MONAD (wasm32 / native / wasi — one core, many adapters)
Kernel loaded at 0x0000, host interface attached
Session initialized. Waiting for console attachment.
Console attached via adapter (web/WASI). Exec ready.
MONAD ready. Type help for available commands.

fortune: A monad is an indivisible unit of being. This one is mine.
The best error message is the one the user never sees.
Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability. — Edsger W. Dijkstra
A kernel is not a program. It is a question that has learned to answer itself.
There are two ways to write error-free code; only the third one works.
Make it correct, make it clear, make it concise, make it fast. In that order.
The contained is always simpler than the container.
A monad is just a monoid in the category of endofunctors.
Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place.
The purpose of abstraction is not to be vague, but to create a new semantic level.
Perfect is the enemy of shipped.
Any sufficiently complicated program contains an ad-hoc, informally-specified bug-ridden slow implementation of half of Common Lisp.
